---
title: "cs"
generated: true
description: "Compound symmetry (exchangeable) within-group correlation."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.lme`](../nitrix-stats-lme.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats.lme._corr`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:cs -->
```python
cs() -> CorrSpec
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:cs -->
Compound symmetry (exchangeable) within-group correlation.

Every pair of within-group observations shares one constant off-diagonal
correlation $\rho$, giving
$R = (1 - \rho) I + \rho\, 1 1^{\top}$ (the `nlme` `corCompSymm`
structure).  It is whitened by the rank-one transform
$w = a z + (b - a)\, \operatorname{mean}(z)\, 1$ with
$a = (1 - \rho)^{-1/2}$ and $b = (1 + (n_i - 1)\rho)^{-1/2}$.
Here $\rho \in (0, 1)$ via $\operatorname{sigmoid}$ (the common
positive-correlation regime; the small negative range admissible for finite
`n` is not exposed).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:cs -->
**Parameters**

*No parameters.*

**Returns** `CorrSpec`. A correlation structure whose whitener applies the rank-one compound symmetry transform, with $\log|R_i| = (n_i - 1)\log(1 - \rho) + \log(1 + (n_i - 1)\rho)$ per group.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
